candidates.why: Explicit D&A summary line exists above EBIT, but it is presented as a separate operating line and is not specifically tied to cost of materials / cost of sales. Given the selected COGS bucket consists of direct material/project-cost rows, adding this broad D&A row to COGS would risk misclassification and double counting.

Component of the broader 'Depreciation and amortisation' summary row. No indication it is specifically a COGS/COR component; using it for COGS would be less appropriate than the summary and risks misallocation.
Component of the broader D&A summary row and not specifically linked to COGS. No evidence it should be separately added to the selected COGS rows.

variables.notes: The selected COGS bucket is formed by direct cost rows ('Cost of materials and other project costs' and 'Material Expenses - Materials, Inventory, Own Work & Utility'). The excerpt shows no COGS-specific D&A line such as 'Depreciation in COGS/COR'. The visible D&A rows are separate operating lines at a broader level, so they should not be added to COGS.

candidates.why: Best summary D&A row above EBIT. It appears as a separate operating line alongside 'Operating expenses', so it is likely not embedded in the selected XSGA bucket and should be added separately. Preferred over component rows to avoid double counting.
This is a component of the separate summary row 'Depreciation and amortisation'. Do not select when the summary row is available.
Also a component of the separate summary D&A row. Exclude in favor of the summary row to avoid double counting.

variables.notes: OK. XSGA_COMPONENTS previously selected 'Operating expenses'. In the excerpt, 'Depreciation and amortisation' is shown as a distinct operating line before EBIT rather than as an apparent sub-component nested under 'Operating expenses'. Under the summary-vs-components rule, select only the summary D&A row.
